---
title: BTree leaderboard
description: Store ordered entries with BTreeStore and read a leaderboard page.
---

## BTreeStore leaderboard

**Focus:** one persistent leaderboard contract that stores score keys in order and reads the highest scores.

[Open in Playground](https://playground.xelis.io/?%%3D%3D)

```rust filename="leaderboard.slx" copy
const BOARD_NS: bytes = "leaderboard".to_bytes()

struct ScoreSubmittedRpcEvent {
    player: Address,
    score: u64
}

struct LeaderboardPageRpcEvent {
    entries: any[],
    total: u64
}

fn score_key(score: u64, player: Address) -> bytes {
    let key: bytes = score.to_be_bytes()
    key.extend(player.to_bytes())
    return key
}

entry submit_score(score: u64) -> u64 {
    let player: Address = Transaction::current().unwrap().source()
    let board: BTreeStore = BTreeStore::new(BOARD_NS)

    board.insert(score_key(score, player), [player, score])
    fire_rpc_event(30, ScoreSubmittedRpcEvent { player, score })
    return 0
}

entry top(limit: u32) -> u64 {
    let board: BTreeStore = BTreeStore::new(BOARD_NS)
    let (cursor, first): (BTreeCursor, optional<Entry>) = board.seek(
        u64::MAX.to_be_bytes(),
        BTreeSeekBias::LessOrEqual,
        false
    )

    let results: any[] = []
    let entry: optional<Entry> = first
    let count: u32 = 0

    while count < limit && entry != null {
        let item: Entry = entry.unwrap()
        results.push(item.value)
        entry = cursor.next()
        count += 1
    }

    fire_rpc_event(31, LeaderboardPageRpcEvent {
        entries: results,
        total: board.len()
    })
    return board.len()
}
```

## How it works

- `BOARD_NS` gives this contract a dedicated persistent tree namespace.
- `score_key(score, player)` encodes the score first, then the player address, so keys sort by score.
- `submit_score(score)` stores one `[player, score]` value and emits `ScoreSubmittedRpcEvent`.
- `top(limit)` seeks near the maximum possible score and walks backward by passing `false` for ascending order.
- `LeaderboardPageRpcEvent` returns the selected entries plus `board.len()` so clients can page the leaderboard.
